about.description: I am a veterinarian, graduated from FMV-UTL, with over 10 years of experience in small companion animal clinics. I have a special interest in proximity care, focusing on welfare and quality of life, disease prevention, early diagnosis, and treatment. My mission is to provide home veterinary care, bringing comfort to animals and convenience to their owners.

blog.title: Home Vet: Benefits, Care and When to Choose This Service
blog.metaDescription: Discover the benefits of home vet services in Lisbon: less stress for your pet, no travel required and personalised consultations. Find out when to choose this service.

intro: Home veterinary services have been gaining relevance in Portugal, especially in urban settings, where lack of time, the stress associated with travel, and the demand for greater comfort for animals increasingly influence owners' choices.

In this home veterinary consultation model, the vet travels to the animal's home, allowing for a calmer, more personalised assessment tailored to its behaviour in the environment where it feels most at ease.
But in which situations is this type of service most recommended, and what are its main advantages?

content.text: A home vet is a licensed professional who provides animal healthcare in the pet's home environment, including:
This care model eliminates the need to travel to a clinic, and is especially useful for more sensitive animals or owners with mobility or time constraints.
Many animals, especially anxious cats and dogs, associate the carrier and car journey with negative experiences.
At home, the animal:

items: Avoids unfamiliar noises, smells, and other animals

content.text: This allows for a calmer, more accurate clinical assessment.
In veterinary clinics, the shared environment increases the possibility of contact between unvaccinated animals and those that are sick or awaiting diagnosis.
By opting for home visits:

items: Contact with potentially infected animals is avoided
The risk of disease transmission is reduced, especially for puppies and kittens during their initial vaccination period

subsections.heading: 3. Greater comfort and convenience for the owner

content.text: The home service eliminates:
It is particularly useful for people with:

subsections.heading: 4. More complete assessment of the animal's environment

content.text: An often underestimated advantage is that the vet can observe the actual environment where the animal lives.
This allows:

items: Assessing diet, hygiene, and space safety
Understanding behaviours that don't appear in the clinic

subsections.heading: 5. Ideal for elderly or mobility-impaired animals

content.text: Senior animals or those with chronic conditions may struggle with:

items: Getting in and out of carriers

content.text: Home care avoids unnecessary suffering and makes the process more dignified and comfortable.
In terminal situations, home care allows for a more intimate and peaceful moment.
Benefits include:

items: Less stress for the animal in its final moments

sections.heading: When to choose a home vet?

content.text: This service is especially recommended for:

items: Cats that are very stressed by travel
Owners with limited availability for travel

content.text: Despite the advantages, it is important to understand that not all cases can be resolved at home.
Referral to a clinic may be necessary in situations such as:
Both models are complementary.

items: The clinic is essential for advanced diagnosis and surgery
Home visits are ideal for comfort, prevention, and follow-up

content.text: The choice always depends on the animal's clinical needs.
The home vet represents a modern, practical, and more comfortable alternative to the traditional clinic model.
When well applied, it improves the experience for both the animal and the owner, reduces stress, and allows for a more personalised approach to veterinary medicine.
